successCriteria.description: File `src/modules/status/health.model.ts` exists and exports a `HealthStatus` interface with exactly these attributes: `isHealthy: boolean`, `timestamp: Date`, `version: string`, `uptimeSeconds: number`.

File `src/modules/status/version.model.ts` exists and exports a `VersionInfo` interface with exactly these attributes: `version: string`, `buildNumber: string`, `commitHash: string`, `buildDate: Date`.
File `src/modules/status/health.service.interface.ts` exists, imports `HealthStatus` from `./health.model`, and exports an `IHealthService` interface with method `getHealth(): Promise<HealthStatus>`.
File `src/modules/status/version.service.interface.ts` exists, imports `VersionInfo` from `./version.model`, and exports an `IVersionService` interface with method `getVersion(): VersionInfo`.
File `src/modules/status/health.repository.interface.ts` exists and exports an `IHealthRepository` interface with method `checkDatabaseConnection(): Promise<boolean>`.
Jest unit tests exist in `tests/unit/modules/status/` that verify all interfaces and models are importable and that model shapes contain the expected properties.
All new files compile without errors under strict TypeScript (`strict: true`, no implicit any, strict null checks) as confirmed by `tsc --noEmit` or equivalent build check.
No existing files are modified and no runtime behavior changes — the application remains fully functional after this phase.

constraints: Strict TypeScript must be enforced: no implicit any, strict null checks enabled (per tsconfig.json `strict: true`).
Follow the existing module pattern and style conventions from `src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts` and `src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts`.
Use `unknown` with type guards instead of `any` (per HARNESS.json constraint `no-any`).
No hardcoded passwords, API keys, or tokens (per HARNESS.json constraint `no-hardcoded-secrets`).
Do not add `@gestalt/*` packages as project dependencies (per HARNESS.json constraint `no-gestalt-internal-deps`).
Test framework is Jest (per HARNESS.json stack). Test files must match the jest.config.js pattern `**/tests/**/*.test.(ts|js)`.
No existing files may be modified — this phase creates only new files with type definitions and interfaces.
No runtime behavior changes — the application must remain fully functional after this phase.
outOfScope: Phase 2: Health repository implementation — implementing the health repository that checks database connectivity.
Phase 3: Health service and Version service implementations — implementing both service classes for the status module.
Phase 4: Status routes and module index — creating Fastify route handlers for `/health` and `/version` endpoints, plus the module barrel export.
Phase 5: Wire status routes into app.ts — registering the new status routes in the main Fastify application.
Modifying the existing `src/modules/status/index.ts` barrel export to include the new interfaces and models.
Any runtime behavior changes or functional endpoints — this phase creates only type definitions and interfaces.

ambiguities.description: The intent says tests should verify "model shapes are correct" for TypeScript interfaces, which are erased at runtime. The exact testing approach is not specified — whether to create concrete objects conforming to the interfaces and assert on properties, or to use type-level assertions.
options: Create runtime objects conforming to each interface and assert that expected properties exist with correct types
Use type-level compile-time assertions only (e.g., type assertions that fail compilation if shapes are wrong)
Both runtime property checks and compile-time type assertions
